analysis.doc: Synchronized from Linear issue identifier: POL-558

POL-558.title: IMPLEMENT: Surface provider routing evidence in PR body and cover the compatibility-mode selection path
POL-558.body:
## Objective

Close the gap where computed provider-routing evidence (selected provider, selection reason, candidate/anomaly signals) is written only to a local, gitignored run-report and never reaches the actual pull request body or test coverage, and document why Devin is deterministically selected in compatibility mode today.

## Context

Produced by ANALYZE issue POL-557. Repo-analysis inspection confirmed: (a) live dispatch always runs in "compatibility mode" because `execution.routerPolicy.providerRegistry` is unset in `polaris.config.json`, so the sophisticated candidate-scoring router engine (`src/loop/router/engine.ts:123`, `decideWorkerRoute()`) never executes; (b) the legacy fallback (`resolveProviderAndModeLegacy()`, `src/loop/dispatch.ts:271-375`) deterministically returns the first entry of `execution.providerPolicy.worker.providers` (`["devin","copilot","codex","kiro"]` in `polaris.config.json:88-93`) with `selection_reason: "role-policy"`, because `execution.rotation` is empty and no persisted rotation/round-robin state exists anywhere — this is documented, intentional static-ordering behavior (`smartdocs/specs/active/worker-router-architecture.md:47,366-402`), not a bug in the ordering itself; (c) the POL-466/467/468/469/494/496/498 routing-evidence work computes a rich `ProviderRoutingSummary` and monopoly-anomaly signals, but only renders them into the local `.polaris/runs/run-report.md` file (`src/finalize/run-report.ts`, `src/finalize/steps/05-generate-report.ts:14,19`) — `src/finalize/github.ts:13-26` (`buildPrBody`) never includes any of it, so the PR a human actually reviews shows none of the routing decision; (d) `src/loop/dispatch.test.ts` has no test asserting the exact `selection_reason === "role-policy"` branch that fires in production (multi-provider role policy + empty rotation).

## Goal

A reviewer looking at the PR body for any Polaris run can see which provider was selected, why (selection_reason/compatibility_mode), and whether a routing anomaly (e.g. provider monopoly) was flagged — without needing to open a local, untracked run-report file. The specific selection branch that fires in production today has direct test coverage, and the static-ordering behavior (vs. rotation/router opt-in) is documented for operators.

## Scope

* src/finalize/github.ts
* src/finalize/github.test.ts
* src/finalize/index.ts
* src/loop/dispatch.test.ts
* src/loop/POLARIS.md

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] PR body includes a routing-evidence section (selected provider, selection reason, compatibility mode, any anomaly signal) sourced from the same data already computed for run-report.md.
- [ ] `src/loop/dispatch.test.ts` covers the "role-policy" compatibility-mode selection path with an explicit assertion.
- [ ] Operator-facing doc explains that provider order in `providerPolicy.<role>.providers` (not rotation) is what's selected unless rotation/router mode is explicitly configured.
- [ ] npm run build and npm test pass.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/finalize src/loop

## Ordering

Cluster is internally ordered — see clusters.json (POL-557 analysis) for child sequencing.

## Non-goals

* Do not change the router engine's candidate-scoring algorithm or enable router mode / providerRegistry by default — that's a policy decision for the user, not this cluster.
* Do not add a rotation/round-robin persistence mechanism — out of scope; the ask is evidence and documentation, not a behavior change to selection itself.
* Do not modify src/loop/router/engine.ts or its tests.

POL-559.title: Surface provider routing evidence in the PR body
POL-559.body:
## Objective

Render the already-computed provider routing summary/anomaly evidence into the actual PR description, not just the local run-report.

## Context

Part of IMPLEMENT parent POL-558 (from ANALYZE POL-557). `src/finalize/github.ts:13-26` (`buildPrBody`) currently only emits Cluster ID, Run ID, Branch, Children completed. The `ProviderRoutingSummary` fields (`selected_provider`, `selected_adapter`, `selection_reason`, `effective_policy_order`, `compatibility_mode`, `registry_present`, `fallback_eligible`) and monopoly-anomaly signals (`src/autoresearch/score.ts` `summarizeRouterOutcomes`/`provider_monopoly_signals`) are already computed for `src/finalize/run-report.ts`'s "Provider distribution"/"Routing review" sections but never passed to `buildPrBody`.

## Goal

`buildPrBody` accepts the routing summary/anomaly data already available at the finalize call site and renders a "Provider routing" section in the PR body mirroring (a condensed form of) what run-report.md shows.

## Scope

* src/finalize/github.ts
* src/finalize/github.test.ts
* src/finalize/index.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `buildPrBody` signature/call site extended to accept routing summary + anomaly data already computed during finalize.
- [ ] PR body includes a "Provider routing" section with selected provider, selection reason, compatibility mode, and any flagged anomaly.
- [ ] New/updated tests in `src/finalize/github.test.ts` assert the section is present and correctly populated from sample data.
- [ ] npm run build and npm test pass.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/finalize/github.test.ts

## Ordering

None — first child in the cluster.

## Non-goals

* Do not change `run-report.ts`'s rendering logic itself — only reuse its already-computed data at the PR-body call site.
* Do not change how routing decisions are computed (`src/loop/dispatch.ts`, `src/loop/router/engine.ts`).

POL-560.title: Add regression test for the compatibility-mode role-policy selection path
POL-560.body:
## Objective

Add direct test coverage for the exact selection branch that fires in this repo's production config today: multi-provider role policy with no `execution.rotation` configured.

## Context

Part of IMPLEMENT parent POL-558 (from ANALYZE POL-557). `src/loop/dispatch.test.ts` covers `config-rotation`, `config-first-provider`, `policy-filtered-rotation`, and `cli-provider-override` branches of `resolveProviderAndModeLegacy()` (`src/loop/dispatch.ts:271-375`), but grepping the test file for the literal `"role-policy"` selection-reason string returns zero matches — the "no rotation configured + multi-provider role policy → first configured provider wins" branch (`dispatch.ts:296-322`), which matches this repo's actual `polaris.config.json`, has no direct assertion.

## Goal

A test explicitly configures `providerPolicy.worker.providers` with 2+ providers and no `execution.rotation`, and asserts `resolveProviderAndMode`/`resolveProviderAndModeLegacy` returns `selection_reason === "role-policy"` and selects the first configured provider.

## Scope

* src/loop/dispatch.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] New test case reproduces the "role-policy" branch (multi-provider policy, empty rotation) and asserts both the selected provider and `selection_reason`.
- [ ] Existing tests in the file remain unchanged and passing.
- [ ] npm run build and npm test pass.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/loop/dispatch.test.ts

## Ordering

None — independent of the other children in this cluster.

## Non-goals

* Do not modify `src/loop/dispatch.ts` itself — this is test-only.
* Do not touch `src/loop/router/engine.ts` or its tests.

POL-561.title: Document compatibility-mode static provider ordering for operators
POL-561.body:
## Objective

Explain, in the route-local operational doc, why a given provider (e.g. Devin) is deterministically selected every run in compatibility mode, and how an operator opts into rotation or full router-mode candidate scoring instead.

## Context

Part of IMPLEMENT parent POL-558 (from ANALYZE POL-557). This behavior is already correctly documented deep in `smartdocs/specs/active/worker-router-architecture.md` (§8.1/§8.4, lines ~366-402) and in doc comments on `execution.rotation`/`providerPolicy` in `src/config/schema.ts`, but there is no operator-facing note in `src/loop/POLARIS.md` (the route-local operational canon for this code) — someone reading dispatch.ts's home route doc has no pointer to why the "same provider every time" behavior is expected absent config changes.

## Goal

`src/loop/POLARIS.md` states plainly: compatibility mode (the default, since `providerRegistry` is unset) always selects the first eligible provider in `providerPolicy.<role>.providers` unless `execution.rotation` is configured; there is no round-robin/persisted rotation state; full candidate scoring only happens in router mode.

## Scope

* src/loop/POLARIS.md

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `src/loop/POLARIS.md` gains a short section (or note in an existing relevant section) explaining compatibility-mode static ordering, with a pointer to `smartdocs/specs/active/worker-router-architecture.md` for full detail.
- [ ] No source code changed in this child.
- [ ] npm run build and npm test pass (sanity check — should be a no-op).

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test

## Ordering

None — independent of the other children; can run in parallel.

## Non-goals

* Do not modify `smartdocs/specs/active/worker-router-architecture.md` or any config schema file.

POL-557.title: ANALYZE: Explain repeated Devin selection and missing routing evidence in live Polaris runs
